content: I just moved to a new city and I'm still figuring out the neighborhoods. Lots of parks and coffee spots to explore.
That sounds exciting. Want to share what you’re enjoying so far? We can also brainstorm low-key ways to get a feel for the area.
Started rock climbing at a local bouldering gym. Still on V1–V2 routes, but it’s a fun way to meet people.
Weekend project: building a cedar planter box for the balcony. Hoping to grow basil and mint. Still deciding on soil and drainage.
Trying to read more fiction this year—I've been mostly non-fiction for ages. Thinking speculative fiction or short novels to start.
Speculative fiction could be a great pivot. If you’d like, I can suggest a few light, engaging options later.
Commute is mostly by bike now. Still learning the best routes without too much traffic.
Made a big pot of veggie soup and froze portions for the week. Simple routines help while settling into the new city.
Rainy weather actually helps me focus—perfect for tinkering with the planter measurements.
On personal topics—I’d rather not get into budgeting details or health stuff. Let’s keep it light for now.
